changelog: v3: Flat power-ranked list (most -> least capable). Only models with reliable native tool-calling are included, since Aspen's assistant depends on tools (time, web search, math). Reasoning-only and non-tool models (deepseek-r1, phi, mistral-nemo) removed. Recommendation is chosen by hardware tier at runtime.

aliases._comment: Maps commercial model names to the best installed local model at or below the given tier. All targets are tool-capable.

models.name: Llama 4 Scout
models.why: Meta's open flagship. 10M-token context, MoE efficiency, strong tool use. Needs serious hardware.

models.name: Qwen 3 32B
models.why: Most reliable tool-calling of any local model. Top-tier quality, rarely drops a tool call.

models.name: Gemma 4 27B
models.why: Native function-calling trained into the weights. Excellent all-round agent model.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 32B
models.why: GPT-4-class quality with a mature tool-calling ecosystem. Great for complex tasks.

models.name: Qwen 3 14B
models.why: Production-grade tool reliability that fits a 16 GB machine. The sweet spot for most users.

models.name: Gemma 4 9B
models.why: Built-in tool calling and vision in a compact size. Runs on modest hardware.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 7B
models.why: Beats many larger models on benchmarks, with solid, well-tested tool support.

models.name: Llama 3.1 8B
models.why: The reliable lightweight pick. Fast, English-first, dependable tool calling.

models.name: Llama 3.2 3B
models.why: Runs on anything. Tool-capable and fast, best when RAM is tight.
